comments.text:
HOW TO BUILD A START UP BY PETER ELIOTT (CEO OF LINK), 08/03/2026

1. The road to hell is paved with premature optimisation.
2. Being an entrepreneur means doing the gruesome nobody else wants to do to solve a problem that no one wants to solve.
3. Leverage yourself and your team to solve everyday problems.
4. Don't be nervous to charge money for your products and services.
5. Listen to your customers and iterate to create better products and services.
6. Don't focus alone on either the problem and/or the solution. Do both. Henry ford said that if he asked people what they wanted, they would have said a faster horse; this is because they wouldn't even have been able to conceptualise a car. You can't build stuff and hope people want it but you can't focus solely on the problem.
7. Never stop innovating.
8. Prioritize your customers wants and needs.
9. Your willingness to solve extremely difficult problems is directly proportional to the amount of value you create within the marketplace.
10. Create an infrastructure company instead of a plugin company if you can or iterate your way there.
11. Intelligence is mildly important, the ability to communicate effectively and understand people is even more important.
